title: Moving zeroization gap from inline TODO to module-level doc is the right call
description: The commit replaces inline ZEROIZATION GAP TODO comments scattered in get() and put() with a single # Zeroization module-level doc section. This is the correct approach: inline TODO comments in production code are invisible to users, confuse contributors, and don't belong in shipped code. A module-level doc section puts the limitation where users will actually see it (rendered docs) and where contributors can find the design constraint without reading implementation code. A future contributor suggesting 'just add Zeroizing wrappers here' should be redirected to the module doc explaining why that doesn't fix the root cause (it's the SDK's internal buffers, not our code).
